mainEntity.name: How much does an electrical panel upgrade cost in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Upgrading from 100A to 200A costs $1,800 to $3,500 in most markets. Upgrading to 400A costs $3,500 to $6,500. These prices include the new panel, breakers, labor, permit, and utility coordination.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to rewire a house?

acceptedAnswer.text: A whole-house rewire for a 1,500-sq-ft home costs $8,000 to $15,000. For a 2,500-sq-ft home, expect $12,000 to $22,000. The main cost drivers are home size, number of circuits, wall construction, and access to wiring paths.

mainEntity.name: How much does EV charger installation cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A Level 2 (240V, 50A) EV charger installation costs $800 to $2,200, including the charger unit, dedicated circuit, wiring, and permit.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a permit for electrical work?

acceptedAnswer.text: In most jurisdictions, yes, for anything beyond replacing a device in an existing box. Panel upgrades, new circuits, rewiring, and generator installations all require an electrical permit and inspection.

mainEntity.name: How long does a whole-house rewire take?

acceptedAnswer.text: A typical rewire takes 5 to 10 days of on-site work, depending on home size and complexity. Total project timeline from start to finish is often 2 to 4 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Should I upgrade to a 400-amp panel?

acceptedAnswer.text: If you are adding or planning to add solar, an EV charger, a heat pump, and a water heater, a 400A panel provides headroom for all of these loads. For homes that are fully electrifying, 400A is increasingly the recommended choice.
